pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates policy file at this exact path (lines 36-84)
setup_task.sh references this path (line 10), removes stale file (line 32)

Policy REQUIREMENT 1 mandates 'SmartScreen Phishing & Malware Protection — ENABLED'. Microsoft Edge uses safebrowsing.enabled = true to control SmartScreen (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-security-smartscreen)

Policy REQUIREMENT 2 mandates 'Built-in Password Manager — DISABLED'. Edge uses credentials_enable_service preference (verified in export_result.sh line 43). Password manager is a real Edge feature that can be disabled (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/passwordmanagerenabled)

Policy REQUIREMENT 3 mandates 'Address and Form Autofill — DISABLED'. Edge uses autofill.enabled preference (verified in export_result.sh line 47). AutoFill is a documented Edge feature (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/deployedge/microsoft-edge-browser-policies/autofilladdressenabled)

Policy REQUIREMENT 4 mandates 'Default Search Engine — DuckDuckGo'. DuckDuckGo (https://duckduckgo.com) is a verified, real search engine with over 100 million searches per day, known for privacy protection (https://duckduckgo.com)

Policy REQUIREMENT 5 mandates 'Tracking Prevention Level — Strict'. Microsoft Edge has three documented tracking prevention levels: Basic, Balanced, and Strict. Strict is the most restrictive option that blocks potentially harmful trackers and most cross-site trackers (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/web-platform/tracking-prevention)

pi_items.metadata_value: IT Administrator / Regulatory Affairs Manager - browser security policy enforcement
pi_items.verified_value: IT Administrator / Regulatory Affairs Manager - browser security policy enforcement
pi_items.source: README.md (lines 3-4) states: 'IT administrators and regulatory affairs managers are frequently required to configure browsers to meet enterprise security policies. This is real professional work.'

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring Microsoft Edge browser settings to comply with a synthetic corporate security policy document. Five mandatory requirements are verified: (1) Microsoft Defender SmartScreen must be ENABLED (controlled by safebrowsing.enabled preference), (2) Password Manager must be DISABLED (controlled by credentials_enable_service), (3) Address Autofill must be DISABLED (controlled by autofill.enabled), (4) Default search engine must be set to DuckDuckGo (a real privacy-focused search engine at https://duckduckgo.com), and (5) Tracking prevention must be set to Strict mode (the most restrictive of Edge's three tracking prevention levels). All five Edge preferences and required values are real, documented features of Microsoft Edge. The task context represents authentic IT administrator work for regulatory compliance.
